message:
test(readme): pin IndexLabel + ValidityMask serde JSON shapes

Sister to fd90.38 (Scalar). Two more documented serializable types
lacked literal-shape assertions — round-trip identity was tested but
the wire format itself was not pinned, so a drift to a different
serde representation could silently break consumer integrations.

Adds readme_indexlabel_validitymask_serde_shape — reachable via
prelude alone — that asserts:

IndexLabel (same #[serde(tag=kind, content=value)] tagged-enum as Scalar):
- Int64(7)        → {"kind":"int64","value":7}
- Utf8("alpha")   → {"kind":"utf8","value":"alpha"}
- Datetime64(ns)  → {"kind":"datetime64","value":1700000000000000000}
- Timedelta64(ns) → {"kind":"timedelta64","value":86400000000000}

ValidityMask (custom Serialize impl):
- 3 values with the middle null → {"bits":[true,false,true]}
- empty mask                    → {"bits":[]}

API discovery (noted): README §1578 says "ValidityMask serializes as
a Vec<bool>" but the actual wire format wraps the bool array in a
struct with a "bits" field. Pinning the actual shape catches any
future drift.

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.39.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
